etymology.description: original pictogram 余(余) (yú, "surplus") -> 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") added for distinction
etymology.interpretation: 余(余) (yú, "surplus") originally represented the character but was ambiguous, so 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") was added as a semantic component to clarify the meaning. The mouth suggests a place for speaking, leading to senses of leaving or giving up as one departs.

examples.chinese: 他舍不得离开家乡(他捨不得離開家鄉)。
examples.english: He is unwilling to abandon his hometown.

examples.chinese: 为了梦想，她舍弃了稳定的工作(為了夢想，她捨棄了穩定的工作)。
examples.pinyin: Wèile mèngxiǎng, tā shěqìle wěndìng de gōngzuò.
examples.english: She gave up a stable job for her dreams.

examples.chinese: 在紧急情况下，人们不会轻易舍命(在緊急情況下，人們不會輕易捨命)。
examples.pinyin: Zài jǐnjí qíngkuàng xià, rénmen bù huì qīngyì shěmìng.
examples.english: In an emergency, people won't easily give up their lives.
